meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Finish Your Student Projects Fast
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your projects. Use this intermediate guide to master Claude Code and Antigravity for faster, professional-grade software development.
content.answer_block: To finish your projects, stop coding in isolation. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you gain an autonomous engineering partner that handles boilerplate and refactoring. This workflow shifts your focus from syntax errors to system architecture, allowing you to ship functional, portfolio-ready applications in half the time.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished GitHub repositories is filled with brilliant ideas that died at the 'boilerplate phase.' You start with high energy, only to get buried under dependency hell, confusing API documentation, and the paralyzing fear that your architecture isn't 'senior' enough. This cycle of starting and stalling is the single biggest barrier to your career growth.

But the game has changed. By leveraging Claude Code as an autonomous agent and Antigravity to scaffold your logic, you stop being a lone coder and start being a Lead Architect. This isn't just about writing code faster; it's about shifting your mindset from struggling with syntax to mastering complex systems. It is time to clear your backlog and ship your first production-ready app.

steps.title: Environment Isolation and Initialization
steps.description: Establish a clean, robust environment to prevent dependency conflicts. This ensures your project remains portable and professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust project structure for a modern SaaS application using TypeScript, including a clear separation between services, controllers, and models.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Logic
steps.description: Define your schema and business requirements clearly so the AI acts as a partner rather than a simple code generator.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these user requirements and generate a highly scalable database schema and API endpoint mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Implement the schema defined in schema.prisma"

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Break massive features into atomic, testable units. Avoid the 'giant PR' trap by shipping one functional component at a time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the 'User Authentication' feature into three distinct, testable PRs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Build the OAuth login flow with Supabase"

steps.title: Automated Debugging and Refactoring
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan for technical debt and optimize inefficient loops or database queries before they become bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for performance bottlenecks and suggest refactoring patterns that follow clean code principles.

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Ensure your project is ready for the world by generating professional documentation and automated CI/CD pipelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that includes architecture diagrams, setup instructions, and API documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated deployment"

pro_tips: Always provide the 'Why' in your prompts; Claude Code performs 30% better when it understands the business goal behind the code.
Use 'claude run' to perform dry-runs before applying complex refactors to ensure you don't break your existing logic.
Treat your Antigravity prompts as 'System Requirements Documents'—the more precise the input, the higher the quality of the output.
Never accept the first iteration; use 'claude chat' to iterate on the logic until it aligns with industry-standard design patterns.
Maintain a 'context file' in your root directory that Claude Code can read to understand your project's evolving global state.
content.next_steps: Once you have your first project running, focus on deploying it to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and share it on LinkedIn to build your professional brand.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects gather digital dust. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap to ship software that actually matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
